ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-24-2017 Continued To Aug-23-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PGN-14-297438 Guardianship of Daniel Joseph Colon, Jr. and PES-14-297806 The Estate of Daniel Joseph Colon.

Appearances: Jennifer Zarich for and with Symone Colon (Guardian).

The Court noted that filing of the receipt for the blocked account is cleared up and that it can go off calendar. The Court granted the Petition to Transfer to Alameda County (subject to review of the order). The Court denied the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel stating that it will allow Alameda County to address this issue as it may have questions. Ms. Zarich requested a 30-day continuance in the Estate matter stating that the petition for final distribution is ready to be filed. Status on close of the estate is continued to August 23, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Reporter: Katy Leonard, CSR #11599, 415-398-1889.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Continued To Jul-24-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Continued To Jun-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Continued To Jun-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Zarich appeared for petitioner Symone Cole.

Ms. Zarich reported that all isseues had been resolved except one, and her client is dealing with the estate accountant to complete and file the final tax return. She stated that it was likely there would be a small tax refund which needed to be included in the final accounting. She requested a continuance of 90 days to allow completion and filing of the tax return, and for the receipt and accounting for of any refund. The matter was continued to June 5, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Inventory & Appraisal
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Inventory & Appraisal
Off Calendar Feb-03-2016 Continued To Feb-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Zarich for and with (administrator) Symone Colon.

Ms. Zarich reported that they filed a corrected Inventory & Appraisal on Monday as to the bank account, confirmed that it was not signed by the probate referee, that they filed a statement regarding the date of death bank statements, that it took a year for Ms. Colon to be appointed as administrator and during that time she had no access to records. The Court stated that the corrected inventory will need to be signed by the probate referee. The status on filing an inventory & appraisal was continued to February 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on filing a completed amended inventory.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Inventory & Appraisal
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Continued To Feb-03-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Zarich for petitioner Symone Colon; Franchesca Callejo as guardian ad litem for Daniel Colon, Jr.

The Court asked for an update on the status of the filing of the inventory & appraisal. Ms. Zarich replied that Mrs. Colon had completed the list of personal property of the estate, which will be provided to the probate referee by the end of next week. She requested a continuance of the status hearing by thirty days to get the inventory on file. The Court stated that Mrs. Colon had failed to discharge her responsibilities as personal representative of the estate in a timely manner, and he was prepared to suspend her as personal representative. Ms. Callejo stated that as guardian ad litem she would like the administrator to be given time to get the inventory on file. She said that Mrs. Colon had been cooperating with her and with Mrs. Colon's counsel, and that the delay was caused by refusal of other members of the deceased's family to cooperate with Mrs. Colon. The Court directed that the opening bank statement be filed with the court by no later than January 27, 2016. The matter was continued to February 3, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Inventory & Appraisal
Off Calendar Nov-16-2015 Continued To Dec-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PGN-14-297438 Guardianship of Daniel Colon Jr. and
PES-14-297806 Estate of Daniel Joseph Colon.
Appearances: Jennifer Zarich for (guardian/administrator) Symone Colon.

The Court asked how much additional time would be needed to file the Inventory & Appraisal in the estate matter. Ms. Zarich stated that she would need 60 days, that they are in triage mode regarding the guardianship, that Ms. Colon has marshaled the property and sent it to the Probate Referee and that some funds were given to the mother of the decedent. The Court stated that it would only grant 30 days and the status of filing the inventory & appraisal was continued to December 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court turned to the guardianship case, stated that it has real concerns and asked why it should not suspend the guardian. Ms. Zarich stated that Ms. Colon has hired an accountant, that they have received a balance sheet from the accountant, that they need to speak to the accountant regarding the format, that the only funds spent and needing accounting for were SSI Survivor Benefits, that the pension funds came in after the accounting date and that the only funds spent were for tuition. The Court stated that it is suspending Ms. Colon as guardian, that it expects the supplemental documents to be on file by December 21, 2015 and that that will be the first pre-condition to have Ms. Colon reinstated. The status on filing an accounting, compliance status hearing, status on filing additional bond or receipts into a blocked account, request to pay entire year's tuition, petition to transfer to Alameda County and the status of filing receipt for deposit of funds into a blocked account were continued to December 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Zarich for and with (petitioner) Symone Colon.

Ms. Zarich stated that they have withdrawn their request regarding Ms. Colon's address and filed a declaration providing the address to the court. The court stated that all competing petitions and objections have been withdrawn and that the petition for letters of administration is granted (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Cole and Jennifer Zarich for and with Symone Colon. Paul Smoot for and with Christine Colon.

The Court stated that it was wondering if Estate administration in necessary depending on how title of the house is held. Mr. Smoot stated that Christine has withdrawn her petition to administer the Estate because of the allegation that she owes $80,000.00 and that the only issue is her objection to Symone administering the Estate. Counsel indicated that they have discussed mediation and were considering Bette Epstein, and spoke of the Decedent's personal belongings being in possession of the family members. Ms. Zarich requested that her Client be appointed stating that she has statutory authority. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 18, 2014. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 18, 2014. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to and the Parties are to return on September 23, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
